1_vs_2.note: F32 single-token decode vs F16 multi-token; storage precision delta, not computational

2_vs_4.note: FIRST CLEAN COMPUTATIONAL DRIFT — both NP=2 and NP=4 use F16 multi-token storage; byte-identical through layers 0-18; layer 19 is the first FA layer where drift breaks F16 precision

2_vs_8.note: max_abs_diff at layer 0 ≈ 1e-6 (F32 epsilon, below F16 precision); cumulative drift through DeltaNet pipeline at different batch widths

4_vs_8.note: max_abs_diff at layer 0 ≈ 1e-6 (F32 epsilon); same nature as 2 vs 8

headline_finding: PRIMARY drift between NP=2 and NP=4 first amplifies above F16 precision at LAYER 19 (full_attention). DeltaNet recurrence kernel itself is not the source — layers 0-18 are byte-identical NP=2↔NP=4. The user's working hypothesis that 'DeltaNet is non-deterministic at np>1' is overturned: the FA path is the dominant drift amplifier at the NP=2/4 transition documented in T9.1. Layer 19's full_attention op is the D3 target.
secondary_finding: Sub-F16-precision drift exists at layer 0 for ALL non-{2,4} NP transitions (1 vs anything, 2 vs 8, 4 vs 8). Source is upstream of DeltaNet's recurrence kernel — most likely the RMSNorm / Q/K/V projection GEMMs feeding into DeltaNet. Below F16 storage precision so doesn't show byte difference at NP=2 vs NP=4 storage path, but is a real fp32 computational difference present at all non-byte-equivalent NP transitions.

d3_targets.primary: Layer 19 full_attention ops at NP=2 vs NP=4: instrument FA dispatcher (fattn-common.cuh) + Q/K/V projection GEMMs + output projection at this specific layer. The byte-identity break at exactly this layer suggests the FA kernel template / tile geometry pick differs at n_tokens=4 vs n_tokens=2 (PHASE45 D10.e + DFlash T9 hint: Turing m16n8k8 tile, 4 hits new tile boundary).
d3_targets.secondary: Layer 0 NP=4 vs NP=8 (or NP=2 vs NP=8) sub-F16 drift: instrument RMSNorm + Q/K/V GEMMs in DeltaNet's input pipeline at varying n_tokens

files_per_run: 64 transformer layers × N slots = 64*N .bin files (each n_embd=5120 floats); manifest JSON
